description: Pattern-based prevention rules for regression detection (added PREVENT-PI-* rules for TypeScript/pi extensions)

rules.name: JSON.parse without null check
rules.message: Direct property access on JSON.parse result without null check
rules.suggestion: Add null check: const data = JSON.parse(...); if (data) { ... }

rules.name: SQL injection risk via string concatenation
rules.message: Potential SQL injection: String concatenation in SQL query
rules.suggestion: Use parameterized queries or prepared statements

rules.name: Hardcoded credentials
rules.message: Possible hardcoded credential detected
rules.suggestion: Use environment variables or secret management system

rules.name: Godot: Direct .free() on Node
rules.message: Direct .free() on Node can crash. Use queue_free() instead.
rules.suggestion: Use node.queue_free() for safe deferred deletion

rules.name: Godot: Absolute resource paths
rules.message: Absolute file path detected. Use res:// paths in Godot.
rules.suggestion: Replace with res://path/to/resource

rules.name: Godot: String-based get_node()
rules.message: String-based get_node() is fragile. Use @onready with $ or % syntax.
rules.suggestion: Use @onready var node = $Path or %UniqueName

rules.name: Python: Bare except clause
rules.message: Bare except catches SystemExit and KeyboardInterrupt. Use except Exception: at minimum.
rules.suggestion: Use 'except Exception as e:' or catch specific exceptions

rules.name: Python: Mutable default arguments
rules.message: Mutable default argument (list/dict/set) — shared across calls, causes subtle bugs
rules.suggestion: Use None as default and initialize inside the function

rules.name: Go: Ignored error return
rules.message: Error return value ignored. Always check errors in Go.
rules.suggestion: Handle the error: if err != nil { return err }

rules.name: Go: goroutine without context
rules.message: Goroutine launched without context — cannot be cancelled or tracked
rules.suggestion: Pass context.Context to goroutine for cancellation

rules.name: TypeScript: any type usage
rules.message: Usage of 'any' type defeats TypeScript's type safety
rules.suggestion: Define a proper interface or use 'unknown' with type narrowing

rules.name: TypeScript/JS: console.log in production
rules.message: console.log left in production code
rules.suggestion: Remove console.log or replace with proper logging library

rules.name: Rust: unwrap() in non-test code
rules.message: unwrap() can panic. Use proper error handling in production.
rules.suggestion: Use match, ?, or expect() with a descriptive message

rules.name: Docker: latest tag in production
rules.message: Using :latest tag is non-deterministic. Pin to a specific version.
rules.suggestion: Pin image version: FROM node:22.1.0-alpine

rules.name: Shell: Command injection via unsanitized input
rules.message: Potential command injection via string concatenation
rules.suggestion: Use array form for subprocess or shlex.quote() for shell commands

rules.name: Kotlin/Java: Thread.sleep()
rules.message: Thread.sleep() is fragile. Use coroutines (delay) or proper synchronization.
rules.suggestion: In Kotlin use delay(), in Java use CompletableFuture or ScheduledExecutor

rules.name: GDScript: Heavy operations in _process
rules.message: File I/O or resource loading detected in _process. Move to _ready() or background thread.
rules.suggestion: Preload resources in _ready() or use ResourceLoader.load_threaded()

rules.name: AWS/Azure/GCP: Hardcoded cloud credentials
rules.message: Cloud provider access key detected in source code
rules.suggestion: Use IAM roles, managed identities, or environment variables

rules.name: GDScript: Missing type hints
rules.message: Untyped variable declaration. Use 'var name: Type = value' for clarity.
rules.suggestion: Add explicit type: var speed: float = 200.0

rules.name: All: TODO/FIXME/HACK without ticket reference
rules.message: TODO/FIXME without ticket reference — will be forgotten
rules.suggestion: Add ticket reference: TODO(#123) or FIXME(RAD-456)

rules.name: OWASP A03: Command injection risk
rules.message: Potential command injection via eval/setTimeout with string concatenation
rules.suggestion: Use function references instead of strings: setTimeout(() => { ... }, 0)

rules.name: OWASP A05: Debug mode in production
rules.message: Debug mode enabled in non-test code
rules.suggestion: Use environment variables: DEBUG = os.environ.get("DEBUG", "false").lower() == "true"

rules.name: OWASP A05: CORS wildcard
rules.message: CORS wildcard detected — allows any origin to access the API
rules.suggestion: Specify allowed origins explicitly instead of using wildcard

rules.name: AI: Hallucinated package import
rules.message: Triple-underscored package name may be AI-hallucinated. Verify it exists on the registry.
rules.suggestion: Verify the package exists: pip search, npm view, cargo search, etc.

rules.name: Security: Weak hash for passwords
rules.message: Weak hash algorithm used for passwords. Use bcrypt, scrypt, or argon2.
rules.suggestion: Use bcrypt, scrypt, or argon2 for password hashing

rules.name: Security: SSRF via unvalidated URL
rules.message: Potential SSRF: User input passed to URL fetch without validation
rules.suggestion: Validate URL against allowlist before fetching

rules.name: Docker: Missing .dockerignore
rules.message: No .dockerignore file found — entire context sent to Docker daemon
rules.suggestion: Create .dockerignore to exclude .git, node_modules, __pycache__, .env

rules.name: GDScript: Missing type hints in function return
rules.message: Function without return type hint. Use func name() -> ReturnType:
rules.suggestion: Add return type: func get_score() -> int:

rules.name: Dropping messages without anchor-floor guard
rules.message: Dropping a prefix of messages (messages.slice(0, N)) must preserve the last N user messages (anchor-floor) — use boundary.dropBefore() which applies the guard. Fires only when no anchor logic is present.
rules.suggestion: Use src/boundary.ts dropBefore(messages, keepFrom, anchorUserMessages) so the anchor floor + tool-pair invariants are enforced.

rules.name: Splitting a toolCall/toolResult pair at a boundary
rules.message: Compaction boundary must not split an assistant toolCall from its following toolResult (OpenAI-compat 400). The engine guard lives in src/boundary.ts; runtime drops in extensions/ must use it.
rules.suggestion: Use src/boundary.ts computeDropRange/dropBefore which walks the boundary back if the first preserved message is a ToolResult without a preceding ToolUse.

rules.name: Injecting compacted context as role:system
rules.message: pi Message has no 'system' role. Inject compacted context via before_agent_start systemPrompt, not a system message.
rules.suggestion: Return { systemPrompt } from before_agent_start, or prepend a UserMessage.

rules.name: Network calls in extension (must stay local)
rules.message: pi-mega-compact must run fully local — no remote MCP server or network calls at runtime.
rules.suggestion: Keep compaction + vector store on local disk; remove network calls. (Config may still read a URL, but runtime must not fetch.)
